title: The Eva 3010 Title
wikitext:
[]: The Eva 3.0+1.0 Title             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Sep 20, 2014 8:03 pm So, I re-watched Anno’s first directorial work this evening Gunbuster: Aim for the Top , and had a sudden realization as to where Anno got his title "Evangelion 3.0+1.0." SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: The Eva 3.0+1.0 Title             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Sep 20, 2014 8:38 pm Nice catch. (I feel incredibly inadequate that such a weird enumeration never popped out at me all the times I've watched Gunbuster .) FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I wonder if Anno isn’t going to continue digging into his Gunbuster roots with Eva 3.0+1.0.

[]: Re: The Eva 3.0+1.0 Title             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sat Sep 20, 2014 8:40 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I wonder if Anno isn’t going to continue digging into his Gunbuster roots with Eva 3.0+1.0. The title is already there, with the “X+1” numerology in the title.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Sep 20, 2014 11:01 pm Silly theory time~! So we have plenty of documentation of Anno reusing stuff. The man knows what he likes and if he likes it enough he'll dig it up and do it bigger. We've also had plenty of speculation about what he could/might reuse with Gunbuster or digging deeper. I speculated how he might go even further back and reuse Daicon stuff But now we have a potential wrench thrown into the mix: Anno's retrospective at the Tokyo Film Festival Anno said it was Producer Suzuki that asked him about it since Miyazaki retired and can't be bothered. So now that every little thing Anno ever did is about to be displayed for a substantial audiece, what if Anno starts having doubt about reaching back and borrowing again? Sorta like "It was fine when only a few nerds know what I did but now everyone will know where I got that!" Seems like a pretty annoying situation for a creator to find himself in.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Sep 20, 2014 11:07 pm I doubt he's one to compromise his vision based on fan reactions or knowledge. References aren't as fun when nobody gets them, anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sat Sep 20, 2014 11:26 pm Alternatively, going through this retrospective Anno could be reminded of something amazing that he totally forgot about.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Sun Sep 21, 2014 12:03 am The name is gonna be confusing, when the DVD gets out. I hope he changes the name to something more distinct before it comes out. Or else were gonna keep having this conversation. Customer: "I'd like to buy 3.0+1.0 please!" CLERK: "The first and third movie or the fourth movie?"

[]:             Quote Post by LightDragonman » Sun Sep 21, 2014 12:06 am Seriously, I can't help but feel that this title is incredibly pretentious, regardless of references.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 21, 2014 1:01 am Ray wrote: The name is gonna be confusing, when the DVD gets out. I hope he changes the name to something more distinct before it comes out.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Sep 21, 2014 1:17 am That doesn't add up to a round number.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sun Sep 21, 2014 1:41 am My goofy desire for the final movie to be in Black & White is given more credence.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Sep 21, 2014 3:24 am Nice catch-- but what does it mean? Is there anything from that particular episode of Gunbusters that would make sense for us to use to speculate about the fourth movie, given what little we know?

[]:             Quote Post by Jäeger » Sun Sep 21, 2014 4:32 am Ray wrote: The name is gonna be confusing, when the DVD gets out. I hope he changes the name to something more distinct before it comes out. Or else were gonna keep having this conversation. Customer: "I'd like to buy 3.0+1.0 please!" CLERK: "The first and third movie or the fourth movie?"

[]:             Quote Post by xyz346 » Sun Sep 21, 2014 4:43 am You know, that actually gave me a weird thought for the English title... You Can (Not) Redo Alone? Though I doubt that would be the actual one when it comes out for real...

[]: Re: The Eva 3.0+1.0 Title             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Sun Sep 21, 2014 6:57 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: So, I re-watched Anno’s first directorial work this evening Gunbuster: Aim for the Top , and had a sudden realization as to where Anno got his title "Evangelion 3.0+1.0." SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Sep 21, 2014 7:27 am Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Nice catch-- but what does it mean? Is there anything from that particular episode of Gunbusters that would make sense for us to use to speculate about the fourth movie, given what little we know?

[]: Re: The Eva 3.0+1.0 Title             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Sun Sep 21, 2014 10:44 am Guy Nacks wrote: Well, GunBuster is the only major Anno work that hasn't been given some sort of overt callback or reference in the NTE series thus far. EDIT: Ninja'd by the 'chu.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Sun Sep 21, 2014 12:40 pm @ xyz346 You're not the only one to have that thought, look at the Official Eva Final updated NTV preview thread if you get a chance.

[]: Re: The Eva 3.0+1.0 Title             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 21, 2014 12:42 pm I notice that folks are still stubbornly calling the last film "Final", when in fact "Final" was a placeholder that has now been replaced with "3.0+1.0". :p There is always "Shin", you know. Shinoyami65 wrote: Come to think of it, the space battle at the beginning of Q may or ay not have been Gunbuster-inspired. Although Shinji does not receive much of a 'Welcome Home!'

[]: Re: The Eva 3.0+1.0 Title             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Sep 21, 2014 1:27 pm Reichu wrote: There is always "Shin", you know.
